meta_title: For Students: deployment workflow to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Deployment Workflow: Break Through SaaS Development Plateaus
content.seo_description: Stop building local prototypes. Master this deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first professional-grade AI application today.
content.answer_block: Moving from local scripts to production requires a robust deployment pipeline. By integrating Antigravity for infrastructure orchestration and Claude Code for automated refactoring, you eliminate manual configuration errors. This workflow ensures your application is scalable, secure, and ready for real-world traffic, bridging the gap between student projects and professional SaaS.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks iterating on your local machine, watching your AI model perform flawlessly in a protected environment. But the moment you consider 'going live,' a paralyzing wall of configuration, environment variables, and cloud provider complexity stops you in your tracks. You aren't just stuck; you are hitting the professional ceiling of a developer who knows how to code but hasn't yet learned how to ship.

This is the critical inflection point in your engineering career. True builders aren't defined by their local environment, but by their ability to translate code into a persistent, accessible service. We are going to dismantle that wall, replacing fear with an automated deployment workflow that treats your infrastructure as code, turning your local repository into a production-ready engine.

steps.title: Infrastructure Blueprinting
steps.description: Define your cloud architecture as code so your environment is reproducible and scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Terraform configuration for a scalable Vercel and Supabase stack optimized for a Next.js AI application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate infrastructure-as-code files for production deployment'

steps.title: Automated Environment Hardening
steps.description: Secure your API keys and secrets by abstracting them into a production-grade environment management system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a security policy script that masks sensitive environment variables during build time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor .env handling to use secure vault injection'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Orchestration
steps.description: Automate the testing and deployment cycle so every git push triggers a verified production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, unit tests, and deployment steps sequentially.

steps.title: Production-Ready Error Handling
steps.description: Implement global telemetry and error logging to ensure you know when a user hits a bug before they report it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a global error boundary and Sentry integration for a React-based AI interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate Sentry error tracking into the main app entry point'

steps.title: Final Deployment Audit
steps.description: Perform a final automated check of your build artifacts to ensure performance optimization and security compliance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a security and performance audit on the generated build artifacts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit build directory for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks'

pro_tips: Always deploy to a staging environment before production to catch environment-specific bugs.
Use feature flags to decouple deployment from release, allowing you to ship broken code safely.
Implement automated database migrations to prevent data corruption during schema updates.
Monitor your API usage costs daily to prevent runaway billing during the deployment phase.
Keep your build logs public during development to debug faster, but sanitize them for production.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on setting up a feedback loop by integrating user analytics and monitoring tools to iterate based on real-world usage.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the blueprint to move beyond the local terminal and into the global internet. The only difference between a student and a software engineer is the courage to deploy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
